question.text: Section 23 of SCRA applies to a person who, not being a member of a recognized stock exchange, wilfully represents to any person that contracts can be entered into through him. What is the nature of this offence?

answer.text: It is a civil offence punishable with fine only
It is a minor violation punishable with warning only
It is an administrative offence with suspension of trading rights
It is a criminal offence punishable with imprisonment up to 10 years, fine up to Rs. 25 crore, or both

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23 states that such a person, on conviction, shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to ten years or with a fine which may extend to Rs. 25 crore or with both. **The Trap:** It is not just a civil or administrative matter; it is a serious criminal offence with substantial penalties. **Cross-Exam Context:** Criminal penalties under securities laws are tested in NISM Series III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Impersonating or misrepresenting oneself as a stock exchange member is one of the most serious offences under SCRA.
